title: Midterms

jokes.text: There is no 'I' in freedom. Freedom is not 'medom.' It's 'wedom.'
jokes.setup: Standard political rhetoric about freedom and unity
jokes.punchline: Bizarre wordplay creating nonsense words 'medom' and 'wedom'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire - takes vapid campaign speak to absurd conclusion with made-up words that sound profound but mean nothing

jokes.text: I visited a dance studio in Hartford, Connecticut. When I was there, I met a brave firefighter in a wheelchair. Back then, we didn't know what HIV positive was. Which meant he had to lose his kidney. He shook my hand and he said, 'You don't remember me, but I am your grandpa.'
jokes.setup: What seems like a typical political anecdote
jokes.punchline: Each detail becomes more nonsensical, culminating in the impossible grandfather reveal
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation of political anecdote absurdity - each sentence adds another layer of impossibility, perfectly capturing hollow campaign stories

jokes.text: I fluffed 'em, now go fuck 'em.
jokes.setup: Political rally context
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual metaphor for political manipulation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Furlong's crude character perfectly - economical and character-specific profanity

jokes.text: It's like a happy Nuremberg.
jokes.setup: Describing the political rally atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Nazi war crime tribunals but 'happy'
jokes.explanation: Stunning dark comparison - three words that perfectly capture political rally mob mentality with historical perspective

jokes.text: Florida... ought to break it off at Jacksonville and row it to Cuba.
jokes.setup: Losing Florida districts in the election
jokes.punchline: Suggesting literally detaching and relocating the state
jokes.explanation: Good political frustration humor - specific geographic detail makes it feel authentic

jokes.text: Are you getting ready to dump a bucket of pig's blood on my head?
jokes.setup: Receiving increasingly bad election news
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Carrie' - expecting horror movie treatment
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits the moment of dread and humiliation

jokes.text: Yeah, you lost, Dale. Oh, my God. And, actually, I was told that you'd been told.
jokes.setup: Consoling what she thinks is a grieving senator
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's accidentally delivering the bad news herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's oblivious insensitivity - the delayed realization is beautifully awkward

jokes.text: No, not for you, Marjorie. In fact, that's probably why you lost the New Jersey fifth.
jokes.setup: Staff member trying to comfort with compliments about campaign trail
jokes.punchline: Selina brutally dismisses and blames her for electoral loss
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Selina's narcissism and cruelty - takes credit for wins, assigns blame for losses

jokes.text: I want a Cartier fucking dildo.
jokes.setup: List of serious policy demands and expanded responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Ends with absurd luxury sex toy demand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with legitimate political demands then veers into absurd luxury vulgarity. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Well, he told my mom he felt worse that time he ate gay Jap raw fish shit.
jokes.setup: Concerned about father's potential stroke symptoms
jokes.punchline: Father's crude, offensive but complex comparison shows he's fine
jokes.explanation: Establishes Amy's father's character through offensive but medically reassuring complexity of speech

jokes.text: Oh, Amy, that's a great sign, because that's a very complex sentence. Unless he's slurring his words.
jokes.setup: Father's crude statement about feeling worse
jokes.punchline: Selina finds medical reassurance in the linguistic complexity of offensive statement
jokes.explanation: Good medical logic applied to inappropriate content - Selina's practical assessment of stroke symptoms

jokes.text: Yeah, this is you, Gary... 'Dana, Dana, Dana, Dana.'
jokes.setup: Gary asking if he talks about his girlfriend too much
jokes.punchline: Sue mimics his repetitive Dana obsession
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship comedy - Gary's girlfriend obsession is relatable workplace annoyance

jokes.text: So when it hits 2:00 A.M., my eyes will say Holocaust, my mouth will say Carnival.
jokes.setup: Wanting specific lipstick for late-night appearances
jokes.punchline: Contrasting exhausted eyes with bright lipstick using Holocaust imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor contrasting historical tragedy with festive makeup - captures political performance vs reality

jokes.text: Oh, that's 'cause your laptop is still running on Windows 2000 and shit.
jokes.setup: Complaining about sketchy internet connection
jokes.punchline: Blaming outdated operating system
jokes.explanation: Basic tech humor - establishes Jonah's know-it-all personality but joke is predictable

jokes.text: Tonight there's gonna be a plane crash shunting a train wreck onto a crowded highway.
jokes.setup: Describing the election night disaster
jokes.punchline: Multiple disaster metaphors cascading into each other
jokes.explanation: Excellent disaster metaphor escalation - each catastrophe makes the next worse, perfect for election night

jokes.text: Not money problems, money challenges.
jokes.setup: Asked about having money problems
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reframing using corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Jonah's pretentious way of avoiding admitting problems

jokes.text: Oh, ker-chung, ker-ching. Pay up.
jokes.setup: Betting on when politician mentions war record
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Chung' name with cash register sounds
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining politician's name with money sounds - shows Dan's cynical gambling nature

jokes.text: That and the big quake hasn't yet hit San Francisco.
jokes.setup: Asked for something positive about election night
jokes.punchline: Natural disaster that hasn't happened yet counts as good news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent character moment - finding silver lining in absence of catastrophe shows his cold data-driven perspective

jokes.text: Like she's ever gonna need that. I mean, she's not ugly, but she's got a lot of security.
jokes.setup: Confusing rape alarm for something else on eBay
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate assessment of VP's rape alarm needs
jokes.explanation: Shows Mike's inappropriate boundary issues - analyzing boss's attractiveness and safety needs

jokes.text: I have a cracked keel, I have to dry-dock it. There's a bilge, which I don't even know where the fuck...
jokes.setup: Explaining his boat problems
jokes.punchline: Getting lost in nautical terminology he clearly doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Mike in over his head with boat ownership, using terms he doesn't understand

jokes.text: I'd like to be cryogenically suspended. Yeah, and then be woken up in the future? No, never wake up. Just stay suspended.
jokes.setup: Wanting to escape current situation through cryogenics
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he wants permanent unconsciousness, not future revival
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Ben's depression leads to wanting eternal unconsciousness rather than sci-fi resurrection

jokes.text: Yeah, he's got ice in his semen.
jokes.setup: Agreeing Kent is cold
jokes.punchline: Graphic biological metaphor for emotional coldness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visceral metaphor for Kent's coldness - Ben's crude poetry perfectly captures the character

jokes.text: You know that portrait with the guy who looks like a fat Wolverine? It's just one door on the left from there.
jokes.setup: Giving directions to Kent's office
jokes.punchline: Using portrait description comparing historical figure to comic book character
jokes.explanation: Funny visual description mixing historical portraiture with pop culture - practical but irreverent

jokes.text: My good friend POTUS, my Gamma Chi brother, is gonna summon me to his office and he's gonna show me a sword and he's gonna tell me to take the sword and slide it down my throat until it comes out my ass.
jokes.setup: Expecting to be fired by the president
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor of ritualistic suicide with impossible anatomy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark metaphor combining seppuku with fraternity background and impossible physics - Ben's eloquent despair

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, it's in the office next to your Klonopin and feminine itch powder.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about lipstick location
jokes.punchline: Lists VP's private medical supplies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Mike's inappropriate knowledge of boss's personal medical items

jokes.text: Kent Davison? The Pol Pot of pie charts.
jokes.setup: Kent Davison being mentioned as returning
jokes.punchline: Comparing statistician to genocidal dictator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comparison - connecting data visualization to mass murder shows Kent's ruthless nature through Sue's dry wit

jokes.text: You know what they call guys like you in prison? Sweetmeat.
jokes.setup: Threatening Dan with prison
jokes.punchline: Using prison rape terminology
jokes.explanation: Standard prison threat humor - establishes Furlong's crude intimidation style

jokes.text: You're gonna have to be prepared to be gay for the stay 'cause you're going down.
jokes.setup: Continuing prison threats to Dan
jokes.punchline: Prison survival phrase with legal double meaning
jokes.explanation: Crude wordplay combining prison culture with legal consequences - typical Furlong intimidation

jokes.text: Am I supposed to ask a question now? Oh, sure. If you want. Fire away. And what would that question be? Well, that question might be what do you think is the best reposition to take? And what would the answer to that question be? And the answer to that question would be standing right in front of you.
jokes.setup: Kent trying to have a conversation with Selina
jokes.punchline: Bizarre meta-conversation about having a conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Kent's analytical mind can't handle normal conversation, creating recursive dialogue loop

jokes.text: Honey, if you're not at the table, you're probably on the menu.
jokes.setup: Quoting her uncle's political wisdom
jokes.punchline: Cannibalistic metaphor for political vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Great political metaphor using cannibalism - captures power dynamics succinctly

jokes.text: And, Kent, I will not be eaten.
jokes.setup: Following her uncle's cannibalistic political metaphor
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of metaphorical threat
jokes.explanation: Good followup to metaphor - Selina's literal statement of refusal to be consumed

jokes.text: Why would anyone want to eat you?
jokes.setup: Selina's declaration she won't be eaten
jokes.punchline: Kent's literal interpretation missing the political metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent response - his analytical mind completely misses metaphor, asks practical question

jokes.text: Yeah, it's the only thing I'd save if this building were on fire.
jokes.setup: Showing off his oversized coffee mug
jokes.punchline: Values coffee mug over everything else in government building
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kent's priorities - caffeine addiction over governmental responsibility

jokes.text: Why don't you take the first bullet and put it through your brain?
jokes.setup: Selina's inspirational bullet metaphor
jokes.punchline: Suggesting suicide as first military action
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark callback - Ben's suicidal depression turns Selina's military metaphor literal

jokes.text: Well, salt and pepper 'em.
jokes.setup: Kent saying his numbers taste bland
jokes.punchline: Literal cooking advice for mathematical data
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist callback - treating numbers like food continues Kent's weird metaphor

jokes.text: You have as much chance of getting the vice president on your show as you have of getting your husband to leave that cheerleader.
jokes.setup: Rejecting TV show booking request
jokes.punchline: Personal attack on producer's marriage problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Sue moment - professional rejection becomes deeply personal attack with specific detail

jokes.text: It's kind of like dumping all my ex-boyfriends at once.
jokes.setup: Enjoying rejecting media requests
jokes.punchline: Comparing professional duties to personal revenge
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Sue finds professional satisfaction in personal relationship parallels

jokes.text: You can't reason with him. It'd be like explaining Supertramp to a Komodo dragon.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to deal with Kent
jokes.punchline: Comparing rational argument to explaining rock band to reptile
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd comparison - random 70s band and dangerous lizard perfectly captures communication futility

jokes.text: like some sort of thick rubber condom
jokes.setup: Worried Kent will get between her and POTUS
jokes.punchline: Graphic contraceptive metaphor for political interference
jokes.explanation: Good sexual metaphor for political access - Selina wants 'unprotected' contact with president

jokes.text: So put everything on the wagons and shoot the dogs because we have just lost the fucking House.
jokes.setup: Announcing they've lost the House
jokes.punchline: Old West retreat metaphor with animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Great historical metaphor for political retreat - wagon train disaster captures total defeat

jokes.text: Screw you and the face you rode in on, Dan.
jokes.setup: Insulting Dan after losing election
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'horse you rode in on' using Dan's face
jokes.explanation: Decent variation on classic insult - Furlong's crude creativity with familiar phrase

jokes.text: No, I said don't wave it in my face. I didn't say eat it. Eat it! Eat it all right now!
jokes.setup: Dan misunderstanding pizza instruction
jokes.punchline: Furlong forces him to eat pizza he was just complaining about
jokes.explanation: Good power play humor - Furlong's petty dominance through food humiliation

jokes.text: It's not like it's Google.
jokes.setup: Amy works for the vice president
jokes.punchline: Dismissing VP role as less important than tech company
jokes.explanation: Good modern political humor - tech company more prestigious than government office

jokes.text: Oh, God. It's fucking Big Bird.
jokes.setup: Seeing Jonah approaching
jokes.punchline: Comparing tall, awkward Jonah to Sesame Street character
jokes.explanation: Good visual nickname for Jonah - captures his height and childish demeanor

jokes.text: Jesus, I can feel my virginity growing back in here.
jokes.setup: Being in room with statistics nerds
jokes.punchline: Claiming exposure to nerds reverses sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dan line - captures his superficial coolness and fear of appearing uncool around nerds

jokes.text: Is that a racist joke? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking for data 'in English'
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment that it was indeed racist
jokes.explanation: Good cringe meta-humor - Dan's blunt confirmation of obvious racism creates awkward moment

jokes.text: Always with the last word. That's why you're single. Guys hate that. You have three kids by two different guys. Maybe your last word should have been no.
jokes.setup: Sisters arguing at father's bedside
jokes.punchline: Escalating personal attacks about relationship failures
jokes.explanation: Good sibling cruelty - personal attacks escalate from personality to sexual choices

jokes.text: By definition, you should be at half the height of the president.
jokes.setup: Discussing VP's relative position
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'Vice President' meaning half
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent logic - applying mathematical precision to political hierarchy with absurd literalness

jokes.text: Now why don't you just go and fuck yourself in your own asshole?
jokes.setup: Kent's condescending mathematical VP explanation
jokes.punchline: Anatomically impossible sexual insult
jokes.explanation: Good Selina explosion - crude impossibility shows her frustration with Kent's analytical coldness

jokes.text: Fuck that lipstick!
jokes.setup: Gary bringing lipstick during heated argument
jokes.punchline: Explosive rejection of previously crucial cosmetic
jokes.explanation: Great emotional escalation - lipstick that was crucial earlier becomes irrelevant during meltdown

jokes.text: I'm a big part of Gary's life now. And you're welcome to him. I think we both know that any woman who's met him but didn't get to keep him is gonna see me as the enemy. I'm sorry, but you missed your chance.
jokes.setup: Dana meeting Amy for first time
jokes.punchline: Assuming Amy wanted romantic relationship with Gary
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion - Dana's complete misreading of Gary's desirability and Amy's interest

jokes.text: Uzbekistan is between Turkmenistan and I-could-give-a-fuck-istan.
jokes.setup: Briefing about hostage situation in Uzbekistan
jokes.punchline: Fake country name expressing indifference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant geographical wordplay - creating fake Central Asian country to express political apathy

jokes.text: I need that stuff that junkies use. You know, when it takes a cop 15 bullets to put him down.
jokes.setup: Needing energy for morning show marathon
jokes.punchline: Requesting illegal drugs with police violence reference
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - VP requesting hard drugs with violent imagery to survive media appearances

jokes.text: There's a horse coming out of my head.
jokes.setup: Looking at herself in mirror before interviews
jokes.punchline: Bizarre description of hair or headpiece appearance
jokes.explanation: Good absurd visual description - Selina's sleep-deprived perception of her appearance

jokes.text: Much like when Bigfoot got your mom pregnant, resulting in you.
jokes.setup: Calling lipstick incident an accident
jokes.punchline: Comparing to mythical creature conception
jokes.explanation: Good absurd insult - Mike's creative mythology-based attack on someone's appearance

jokes.text: I think your ass is perfect.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about her large ass
jokes.punchline: Gary's inappropriate workplace compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary inappropriateness - crossing professional boundaries with personal physical compliment

jokes.text: Well, we are the United States of America because we are united and we are states and we are of America.
jokes.setup: Trying to make profound political statement
jokes.punchline: Tautological explanation that explains nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent political nonsense - circular logic that sounds profound but means nothing, perfect Selina exhaustion

tmdb_overview: Fresh off successful campaign appearances for midterm elections, Selina sees an opportunity to expand her role, but must first curry favor with Kent Davison, the president's icy senior strategist. Meanwhile, there are new developments in the staff's personal lives.
insight: Season premiere reboots Selina's chaos at 1.6 jokes per minute, sustaining 83-point momentum.
